groups.title: Guitar Group
groups.desc:
Questions 1-6
Complete the form below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect:
Coordinator:                                            Gary $21672$
Level:                                                    $21673$
Place:                                                     the $21674$
                                                              $21675$ Street
                                                                First floor, Room T347
Time:                                                       Thursday morning at $21676$
Recommended website:                         'The perfect $21677$'

questions.analysis: 正确答案是“Mathieson”，因为听力中明确提到“His name's Gary Mathieson”，并且题干已经给出了名字“Gary”，需要填入的是姓氏。通过定位词“Gary”和信号词“coordinator”，考生可以迅速定位到答案所在的句子。
题干问的是课程的级别（Level），在听力原文中，Gary提到这是一个“class for beginners”，即初学者的课程。因此，正确答案是“beginners”。
答案是“college”，因为听力原文明确提到“he decided to book a room at the college in town”，这表明了地点的变化。通过抓住定位词“Place”和信号词“college”，可以准确找到答案。
题干要求填写的是某条街的名字，定位词是“Street”。在听力内容中，COLEMAN明确提到“New Street”，这是直接的答案。虽然WOMAN提到“Lock Street”，但COLEMAN纠正了她，说明正确答案是“New Street”。
答案是“11”，因为听力原文明确提到“now we meet at 11”。学生需要注意听力中的时间变化，并且在听到“Thursday”这个定位词时，集中注意力听紧随其后的时间信息。
题干要求填写推荐网站的名称。通过定位词“website”，我们在听力原文中找到了对应的信号词“website called”，并且在这之后听到了网站的名称‘The perfect instrument’。因此，正确答案是“instrument”。

groups.desc:
Questions 7-10
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

content: keeping time while the teacher is$21679$
often listening to a$21680$of a song
playing single notes and simple tunes
noting things to practise at home

questions.analysis: 答案是“ear”，因为听力原文中明确提到“Some people have an app they use, but others do it by ear”。通过定位词“tuning guitars”和信号词“tune our guitars”，我们可以准确找到答案所在的句子。学生在听力过程中应特别注意这些关键词和信号词，以便快速定位到答案。
第8题的正确答案是“clapping”，因为在听力原文中明确提到Gary通过拍手（clapping）来帮助大家保持节奏（keeping time）。通过抓住定位词“keeping time”和信号词“clapping”，可以准确找到答案。
第9题的正确答案是“recording”。在听力原文中，明确提到“recording of the song and plays it to us first”，这与表格中的“often listening to a【9】of a song”完全对应。因此，答案是“recording”。
答案是“alone”，因为听力原文中明确提到“he sometimes gets us to play one at a time – you know, alone”，这与表格中“playing together, then”相对应。通过定位词“playing single notes and simple tunes”以及信号词“playing together”，可以准确找到答案。

transcript.en: PART 1
WOMAN: Hi Coleman, how are you?
transcript.cn: 女士：嗨，科尔曼，最近好吗？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: Good, thanks.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：很好，谢谢。

transcript.en: WOMAN: I wanted to have a chat with you because our friend Josh told me that you've joined a guitar group and it sounds interesting.
transcript.cn: 女士：我想和你聊聊，因为我们的朋友乔希告诉我，你加入了一个吉他小组，听起来很有趣。

transcript.en: I'd really like to learn myself.
transcript.cn: 我真的很想自学一下。

transcript.en: COLEMAN: Why don't you come along?
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：你为什么不一起来学呢？

transcript.en: I'm sure there's room for another person.
transcript.cn: 我相信还能再加一个人。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Really? So - who runs the classes?
transcript.cn: 女士：真的吗？谁负责这些课程？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: He's called a 'coordinator' - his name's Gary Mathieson.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：他被称为“协调员”，他的名字是加里·马蒂森。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Let me note that down. Gary. How do you spell his surname?
transcript.cn: 女士：让我记下来。加里，他的姓怎么拼？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: It's M-A-T-H-I-E-S-O-N.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：是 M-A-T-H-I-E-S-O-N。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Right, thanks.
transcript.cn: 女士：好的，谢谢。

transcript.en: COLEMAN: He's retired, actually, but he's a really nice guy and he used to play in a lot of bands.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：他其实已经退休了，但人很好，以前参加过很多乐队。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Thanks. So how long have you been going?
transcript.cn: 女士：谢谢，你去多久了？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: About a month now.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：大概一个月了

transcript.en: WOMAN: And could you play anything before you started?
transcript.cn: 女士：开始学之前你会弹些什么呢？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: I knew a few chords, but that's all.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：我会几个和弦，仅此而已。

transcript.en: WOMAN: I'm sure everyone will be better than me.
transcript.cn: 女士：我相信每个人都比我弹得好。

transcript.en: COLEMAN: That's what I thought, too.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：我一开始也是这样想的。

transcript.en: When I first spoke to Gary on the phone, he said it was a class for beginners, but I was still worried that everyone would be better than me, but we were all equally hopeless!
transcript.cn: 我第一次和加里通电话时，他说这是为初学者开设的课程，但我还是担心每个人都会比我强，但我们都一样一窍不通！

transcript.en: WOMAN: Oh, that's reassuring. So where do you meet?
transcript.cn: 女士：那我就放心了，你们会在哪里见？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: Well, when I joined the group, they were meeting in Gary's home, but as the group got bigger, he decided to book a room at the college in town. I prefer going there.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：嗯，当我加入这个小组时，他们在加里的家里集合，但是随着小组越来越大，他决定在城里的大学预定一个房间。我更喜欢去那里。

transcript.en: WOMAN: I know that place. I used to go to tap dancing classes there when I was at secondary school.
transcript.cn: 女士：我知道那个地方。我上中学的时候常去那里上踢踏舞课。

transcript.en: I haven't been since, though and I can't remember what road it's in . . . is it Lock Street?
transcript.cn: 从那以后我就没去过了，我不记得是在哪条路上了，是洛克街吗？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: It's just beyond there at the bottom of New Street near the city roundabout.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：就在那边的新街尽头，在城市环岛附近。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Yes, of course.
transcript.cn: 女士：对，没错。

transcript.en: COLEMAN: The guitar club is on the first floor in Room T347.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：吉他俱乐部在一楼的T347室。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Right. And when do you meet? Is it at the weekend?
transcript.cn: 女士：好的，你们什么时候见面，周末吗？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: We meet on Thursdays. It used to be 10.30 and that suited me well, but now we meet at 11.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：我们每周四见。以前是十点半，我觉得很合适，但现在是十一点。

transcript.en: The class that's in there before us asked if they could have the room for another 30 minutes.
transcript.cn: 在我们之前的同学问他们能不能再占用这个房间30分钟。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Oh, I see. Well, I'd love to come, but I don't have a guitar.
transcript.cn: 女士：哦，我知道了。我很想来，但我没有吉他。

transcript.en: COLEMAN: Well, you can always buy a second-hand one.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：你可以买一把二手吉他。

transcript.en: There's a website called 'The perfect instrument' that sells all kinds of guitars, violins and so on. I'm sure you'll find something there.
transcript.cn: 有个网站叫 "完美乐器"，出售各种吉他、小提琴等。我相信你会在那里找到的。

transcript.en: WOMAN: So what's a typical lesson like with Gary?
transcript.cn: 女士：那么，加里平常的课程是怎么样呢？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: Well, he always starts by getting us to tune our guitars. That takes about five minutes.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：嗯，他总是先让我们给吉他调音。大约五分钟时间。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Uhuh.
transcript.cn: 女士：嗯嗯。

transcript.en: COLEMAN: Some people have an app they use, but others do it by ear.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：有些人会用自己的应用程序，有些人用自己的耳朵听。

transcript.en: Gary goes round and helps them. And while he's doing that, he tells us what he's going to do during the lesson.
transcript.cn: 加里会过去帮助他们。他一边调一边告诉我们上课要做什么。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Right.
transcript.cn: 女士：好的。

transcript.en: COLEMAN: First, we usually spend about ten minutes doing some strumming.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：首先，我们会花十分钟左右的时间来弹奏。

transcript.en: WOMAN: So is that using . . . what are they called . . . plectrums?
transcript.cn: 女士：那是不是用什么，叫拨子？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: No - we just use our thumbs.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：不，我们就用手指。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Much easier.
transcript.cn: 女士：那简单多了。

transcript.en: COLEMAN: Gary reminds us where to put our fingers for each chord and then we play them together.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：加里提醒我们每个和弦的手指位置，然后我们一起弹奏。

transcript.en: Sometimes we all just start laughing because we're so bad at keeping time, so Gary starts clapping to help us.
transcript.cn: 有时我们都会笑起来，因为我们太不会掌控节奏，加里就开始拍手帮助我们。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Do you learn to play any songs?
transcript.cn: 女士：你们会学弹奏任何歌曲吗？

transcript.en: COLEMAN: Yes - we do at least one song with words and chords. I mean that's harder than you think.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：是的，我们至少会弹一首有歌词与和弦的歌。这比你想象的要难一些。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Oh, I'm sure it is!
transcript.cn: 女士：哦，我想是的。

transcript.en: COLEMAN: That part of the lesson takes about 15 minutes.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：这部分大约需要十五分钟。

transcript.en: He often brings a recording of the song and plays it to us first.
transcript.cn: 他通常会把这首歌的录音带过来，先给我们播放一遍。

transcript.en: Then he hands out the song and if there's a new chord in it, we practise that before we play it together - but really slowly.
transcript.cn: 然后，他把歌曲发给我们，如果其中有新的和弦，我们就先练习一下，然后再一起弹奏——但弹得很慢。

transcript.en: WOMAN: Do you do any finger picking?
transcript.cn: 女士：你们会进行指弹练习吗？

transcript.en: COLEMAN:  That's the last ten minutes of the lesson, when we pick out the individual notes from a tune he's made up. It's always quite simple.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：那是课程的最后十分钟，我们从他编的曲子中挑出单个的音符。总是很简单的。

transcript.en: WOMAN:   That must be hard, though.
transcript.cn: 女士：那一定挺累人的。

transcript.en: COLEMAN:  It is, but people like it because they can really concentrate and if we're all playing well, it sounds quite impressive.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：是的，但人们喜欢这样，因为他们可以真正集中精力，而且如果我们都弹得很好，听起来就会很印象深刻。

transcript.en: The only trouble is that he sometimes gets us to play one at a time - you know, alone.
transcript.cn: 唯一的问题是，他有时会让我们自己弹--你知道，单独演奏。

transcript.en: WOMAN:   That's scary.
transcript.cn: 女人：那有些可怕。

transcript.en: COLEMAN:  It is, but I've got used to it now.
transcript.cn: 科尔曼：是的，但我现在已经习惯了。

transcript.en: At the end he spends about five minutes telling us what to practise for the following week.
transcript.cn: 最后他还会花五分钟告诉我们下周要练什么。

transcript.en: WOMAN:   Well, thanks Coleman. I'll go and have a look at that website, I think.
transcript.cn: 女士：谢谢你，科尔曼。我想我会去看看那个网站的。

groups.title: Working as a lifeboat volunteer
groups.desc:
Questions 11-16
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: What made David leave London and move to Northsea?

options.title: A
options.content: He was eager to develop a hobby.

options.title: B
options.content: He wanted to work shorter hours.

options.title: C
options.content: He found his job in website design unsatisfying.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A，因为听力材料明确提到David搬到Northsea是为了追求他对船只的兴趣。这一信息在听力材料中通过“keen on boats”和“pursue that interest”这两个短语得以体现。学生在听力过程中应特别关注这些关键词，并结合题干中的定位词来确定答案。

questions.title: The Lifeboat Institution in Northsea was built with money provided by
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B. a local resident。听力原文明确提到资金是由一个在当地生活了一辈子的慷慨公众成员提供的。通过定位词和信号词的分析，可以帮助学生更准确地找到答案所在的句子，并理解其含义。

questions.title: In his health assessment, the doctor was concerned about the fact that David
questions.analysis: 在健康评估中，医生关心的是色盲问题。听力原文中明确提到医生认为他可能有色盲问题，但最终证明他没有问题。因此，正确答案是A. might be colour blind。

questions.title: After arriving at the lifeboat station, they aim to launch the boat within
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B（six to eight minutes），因为原文明确提到在到达救生艇站后，他们的目标是在另外六到八分钟内将船从码头开出去并驶向海上。题干问的是在到达救生艇站后，他们的目标是在多长时间内启动船只，因此答案是B。

questions.title: As a ‘helmsman’, David has the responsibility of deciding

options.title: A
options.content: who will be the members of his crew.

options.title: B
options.content: what equipment it will be necessary to take.

options.title: C
options.content: if the lifeboat should be launched.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C，因为听力材料明确提到“helmsman”有决定是否发射救生艇的责任。通过抓住关键词和信号词，学生可以更准确地定位到相关信息，并做出正确的选择。

questions.title: As well as going out on the lifeboat, David

options.title: A
options.content: gives talks on safety at sea.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是A。David除了出海救援外，还会对青年团体和航海俱乐部进行关于海上安全的演讲。原文中提到“speak to youth groups and sailing clubs”，这与选项A中的“gives talks on safety at sea”相对应。选项B和C在原文中没有提到David负责这些工作的内容。

groups.title: Which TWO things does David say about the lifeboat volunteer training?
groups.desc:
Questions 17-18
Choose TWO letters, A–E
Write your answers in boxes 17-18 on your answer sheet.

list.title: A
list.content: The residential course developed his leadership skills.

list.title: B
list.content: The training in use of ropes and knots was quite brief.

list.title: C
list.content: The training exercises have built up his mental strength.

list.title: D
list.content: The casualty care activities were particularly challenging for him.

list.title: E
list.content: The wave tank activities provided practice in survival techniques.

collect_option.title: Which TWO things does David say about the lifeboat volunteer training?

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C和E，因为这两个选项在听力材料中都有明确的对应描述，并且与题干中的“training”相关。C选项提到训练增强了他的心理素质，使他在极端情况下不惊慌失措；E选项提到波浪池活动提供了生存技巧的练习。
这道题的正确答案是C和E，因为这两个选项在听力材料中都有明确的对应描述，并且与题干中的“training”相关。C选项提到训练增强了他的心理素质，使他在极端情况下不惊慌失措；E选项提到波浪池活动提供了生存技巧的练习。

groups.title: Which TWO things does David find most motivating about the work he does?
groups.desc:
Questions 19-20
Choose two letters from the A-E
Write your answers in boxes 19-20 on your answer sheet.

list.title: A
list.content: working as part of a team

list.title: C
list.content: being thanked by those he has helped

list.title: D
list.content: the fact that it keeps him fit

list.title: E
list.content: the chance to develop new equipment

collect_option.title: Which TWO things does David find most motivating about the work he does?

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A和B。David在原文中提到他觉得团队合作非常激励人，他形容团队像一个家庭，这在寒冷的夜晚特别有帮助。此外，他还提到在寒冷的月份工作是最有回报的时间，因为那时的事件更严重，他能对结果产生巨大的影响。
这道题的正确答案是A和B。David在原文中提到他觉得团队合作非常激励人，他形容团队像一个家庭，这在寒冷的夜晚特别有帮助。此外，他还提到在寒冷的月份工作是最有回报的时间，因为那时的事件更严重，他能对结果产生巨大的影响。

transcript.en: PART 2
I never really planned to be a lifeboat volunteer when I came to live in Northsea.
transcript.cn: 当我来到诺斯海生活时，我从未想过要成为一名救生艇志愿者。

transcript.en: I'd been working in London as a website designer, but although that was interesting, I didn't like city life.
transcript.cn: 我一直在伦敦做网站设计师，虽然工作很有趣，但我不喜欢城市生活。

transcript.en: I'd been really keen on boats as a teenager, and I thought if I went to live by the sea, I might be able to pursue that interest a bit more in my free time.
transcript.cn: 十几岁的时候，我就非常喜欢船，我想如果我住在海边，也许能在空闲时间更多地追求我的兴趣。

transcript.en: Then I found that the Lifeboat Institution was looking for volunteers, so I decided to apply.
transcript.cn: 后来我发现救生艇协会正在招募志愿者，于是我决定申请。

transcript.en: The Lifeboat Institution building here in Northsea's hard to miss, it's one of the largest in the country.
transcript.cn: 北海的救生艇协会大楼很显眼，它是全国最大的救生艇协会之一。

transcript.en: It was built 15 years ago with funds provided by a generous member of the public who'd lived here all her life.
transcript.cn: 它建于15年前，由一位慷慨的公众成员提供的资金，她在这里生活了一辈子。

transcript.en: As the Lifeboat Institution is a charity that relies on that kind of donation, rather than funding provided by the government, that was a huge help to us.
transcript.cn: 因为救生艇机构是一个依赖捐赠的慈善机构，而不是政府提供的资金，这对我们帮助很大。

transcript.en: When I applied, I had to have a health assessment.
transcript.cn: 当我申请的时候，我必须做一个健康评估。

transcript.en: The doctors were particularly interested in my vision.
transcript.cn: 医生非常关注我的视力。

transcript.en: I used to be short-sighted, so I'd had to wear glasses, but I'd had laser eye surgery two years earlier so that was OK.
transcript.cn: 我以前是近视眼，所以不得不戴眼镜，但两年前我做了激光眼科手术，所以视力没问题。

transcript.en: They gave me tests for colour blindness and they thought I might have a problem there, but it turned out I was OK.
transcript.cn: 他们给我做了色盲测试，他们认为我可能有问题，但结果证明我没事。

transcript.en: When the coastguard gets an alert, all the volunteers are contacted and rush to the lifeboat station.
transcript.cn: 当海岸警卫队收到警报时，所有的志愿者都会得到联系，并迅速赶往救生艇站。

transcript.en: Our target's to get there in five minutes, then we try to get the boat off the dock and out to sea in another six to eight minutes.
transcript.cn: 我们的目标是在五分钟内赶到那里，然后在六到八分钟内将船驶离码头并驶向大海。

transcript.en: Our team's proud that we usually achieve that - the average time across the country's eight and a half minutes.
transcript.cn: 我们团队引以为豪的是，我们通常都能做到这一点--全国的平均时间是八分半钟。

transcript.en: I've recently qualified as what's called a 'helmsman', which means I have the ultimate responsibility for the lifeboat.
transcript.cn: 我最近获得了所谓的 "舵手 "资格，这意味着我对救生艇负有最终责任。

transcript.en: I have to check that the equipment we use is in working order - the crew have special life jackets that can support up to four people in the water.
transcript.cn: 我必须检查我们使用的设备是否正常工作--船员们都有特制的救生衣，最多可支持四人下水。

transcript.en: And it's ultimately my decision whether it's safe to launch the boat.
transcript.cn: 最终由我来决定是否安全放行。

transcript.en: But it's very rare not to launch it, even in the worst weather.
transcript.cn: 但即使在最恶劣的天气下，也很少有不下水的情况。

transcript.en: As well as going out on the lifeboat, my work involves other things too.
transcript.cn: 除了出海救生，我的工作还包括其他方面。

transcript.en: A lot of people underestimate how quickly conditions can change at sea, so I speak to youth groups and sailing clubs in the area about the sorts of problems that sailors and swimmers can have if the weather suddenly gets bad.
transcript.cn: 很多人都低估了海上环境变化的速度，因此我向当地的青年团体和帆船俱乐部讲述如果天气突然变坏，水手和游泳者可能会遇到的各种问题。

transcript.en: We also have a lot of volunteers who organise activities to raise money for us, and we couldn't manage without them.
transcript.cn: 我们还有很多志愿者，他们组织活动为我们筹款，没有他们，我们无法开展工作。

transcript.en: The training we get is a continuous process, focusing on technical competence and safe handling techniques, and it's given me the confidence to deal with extreme situations without panicking.
transcript.cn: 训练是一个持续的过程，重点是技术能力和安全操作技巧，这让我有信心应对极端情况而不惊慌失措。

transcript.en: I was glad I'd done a first aid course before I started, as that's a big help with the casualty care activities we do.
transcript.cn: 我很高兴自己在入职前参加了急救课程，因为这对我们开展伤员救护活动大有帮助。

transcript.en: We've done a lot on how to deal with ropes and tie knots - that's an essential skill.
transcript.cn: 我们还学习了很多如何处理绳索和打绳结--这是一项基本技能。

transcript.en: After a year, I did a one-week residential course, led by specialists.
transcript.cn: 一年后，我在专家的带领下参加了为期一周的住宿课程。

transcript.en: They had a wave-tank where they could create extreme weather conditions - so we could get experience at what to do if the boat turned over in a storm at night, for example.
transcript.cn: 他们有一个造波水池，可以创造极端的天气状况，所以我们可以获得经验，例如如果船在夜间翻覆，在暴风雨中做什么。

transcript.en: Since I started, I've had to deal with a range of emergency situations.
transcript.cn: 自从我开始工作以来，我不得不处理各种紧急情况。

transcript.en: But the work's hugely motivating.
transcript.cn: 但这项工作给了我极大的动力。

transcript.en: It's not just about saving lives - I've learned a lot about the technology involved.
transcript.cn: 这不仅仅是关于拯救生命——我已经学到了很多关于相关技术的知识。

transcript.en: My background in IT's been useful here, and I can use my expertise to help other volunteers.
transcript.cn: 我的 IT 背景在这里很有用，我可以利用我的专业知识来帮助其他志愿者。

transcript.en: They're a great group - we're like a family really, which helps when you're dragging yourself out of bed on a cold stormy night.
transcript.cn: 他们是一个很棒的团队--我们就像一个大家庭，当你在寒冷的暴风雨之夜从床上爬起来时，这对你很有帮助。

transcript.en: But actually, it's the colder months that can be the most rewarding time.
transcript.cn: 但实际上，寒冷的月份才是最有意义的时候。

transcript.en: That's when the incidents tend to be more serious, and you realise that you can make a huge difference to the outcome.
transcript.cn: 这时候的事故往往更加严重，你会意识到你可以对结果产生巨大的影响。

transcript.en: So if any of you listeners are interested. . . .
transcript.cn: 所以，如果各位听众有兴趣. . . .

groups.desc:
Questions 21-24
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: At first, Don thought the topic of recycling footwear might be too
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A. limited in scope。Don在开始阅读文章之前，认为关于回收鞋类的话题内容有限，不足以展开详细讨论。这一点在听力原文中通过“there isn't enough to say about it”明确表达出来。通过定位词“at first, Don thought”和信号词“before I started reading it, I thought”，可以迅速找到对应的答案句并确认答案。

questions.title: When discussing trainers, Bella and Don disagree about

options.title: A
options.content: how popular they are among young people.

options.title: B
options.content: how suitable they are for school.

questions.analysis: 在讨论运动鞋时，Bella和Don的分歧在于它们是否适合在学校穿。Don认为运动鞋看起来太随意，不适合校服，而Bella则认为有些运动鞋看起来很整洁，比旧鞋更适合学生穿。因此，正确答案是B。

questions.title: Bella says that she sometimes recycles shoes because

options.title: C
options.content: they are no longer in fashion.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是B，因为贝拉明确表示她有时会回收一些完好的鞋子，仅仅是因为这些鞋子不再适合她，而不是因为它们不合脚或过时。通过抓住定位词和信号词，学生可以更准确地找到答案所在的句子，并通过排除法来选择正确的选项。

questions.title: What did the article say that confused Don?

options.title: A
options.content: Public consumption of footwear has risen.

options.title: B
options.content: Less footwear is recycled now than in the past.

options.title: C
options.content: People dispose of more footwear than they used to.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是B，因为Don在听力中明确表示对“the amount of recycled footwear has fallen”感到困惑。通过定位词“confused Don”和信号词“doesn't seem to make sense”，可以准确找到对应的答案句，并确认答案。学生在听力过程中应特别注意这些关键词和反应词，以便快速定位和理解相关信息。

groups.title: Reasons
groups.desc:
Questions 25-28
What reasons did the recycling manager give for rejecting footwear, according to the students?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–F, next to Questions 25–28.

list.title: B
list.content: the colour of one shoe had faded

list.title: C
list.content: one shoe had a hole in it

list.title: F
list.content: the stitching on the shoes was broken

questions.title: the high-heeled shoes
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是E（the shoes were too dirty），因为原文明确提到高跟鞋由于在湿地里穿过，鞋跟被泥和草弄脏，无法再销售。通过定位词“high-heeled shoes”和信号词“ladies' high-heeled shoes”，学生可以迅速找到相关信息，并通过细节描述确定正确答案。

questions.title: the ankle boots
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 B. the colour of one shoe had faded。通过分析听力原文，可以确定答案句为“one of the shoes was a much lighter shade than the other one”，其中的关键词“lighter shade”直接对应了选项B中的“the colour of one shoe had faded”。题干中的定位词“the ankle boots”帮助我们快速定位到相关信息，而信号词“leather ankle boots”则进一步确认了答案的准确性。

questions.title: the baby shoes
questions.analysis: 根据听力原文，婴儿鞋被拒绝的原因是因为其中一只鞋子丢失了。BELLA提到“you'd think it would have been easy to find the other, but it wasn't”，这表明另一只鞋子没有找到，因此选项A是正确的。

questions.title: the trainers
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 C. one shoe had a hole in it，因为听力原文明确提到“one of the soles was so worn under the foot that you could put your finger through it”，这表明鞋底有洞。通过定位词“the trainers”以及信号词“worn”、“put your finger through it”，可以准确找到对应的答案句并理解其含义。

groups.desc:
Questions 29-30
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Why did the project to make ‘new’ shoes out of old shoes fail?

options.title: A
options.content: People believed the ‘new’ pairs of shoes were unhygienic.

options.title: B
options.content: There were not enough good parts to use in the old shoes.

options.title: C
options.content: The shoes in the ‘new’ pairs were not completely alike.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C。对话中明确指出，项目失败的原因是因为新鞋的两只鞋子不完全一样，而不是因为卫生问题或材料不足。

questions.title: Bella and Don agree that they can present their topic
questions.analysis: 在对话中，Bella提到他们可以通过采取一种非常不同的方法来展示他们的主题，Don对此表示完全同意。因此，他们同意以一种新的角度来展示他们的主题，这与选项A“from a new angle”相符。

transcript.en: PART 3
BELLA: Hi Don - did you get the copy of the article on recycling footwear that I emailed you?
transcript.cn: 贝拉：嗨，多恩。你收到我发给你的那篇关于回收鞋子的文章了吗？

transcript.en: DON: Yeah - it's here . . . I've had a look at it.
transcript.cn: 多恩：收到了，在这。我看一下。

transcript.en: BELLA: So do you think it's a good topic for our presentation?
transcript.cn: 贝拉：你觉得这个话题适合我们课堂展示吗？

transcript.en: DON: Well, before I started reading it, I thought recycling footwear, well, although it's quite interesting, perhaps there isn't enough to say about it, cos we put shoes in recycling bins, they go to charity shops and that's about it.
transcript.cn: 多恩：读之前，我认为尽管回收鞋类的话题很有趣，但是也许没有那么多可以说的，因为我们把鞋子放进回收箱里，送到慈善商店，仅此而已。

transcript.en: BELLA: but there's much more to it than that.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：但远不止这些。

transcript.en: DON: I realise that now and I'm keen to research the topic more.
transcript.cn: 多恩：我现在发现了，而且我很想对这个话题进行更多的研究。

transcript.en: BELLA: That's great.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：那太好了。

transcript.en: DON: One of the things I didn't realise until I read the article was just how many pairs of trainers get recycled!
transcript.cn: 多恩：直到读了这篇文章，我才意识到有多少双运动鞋被回收利用！

transcript.en: BELLA: Well, a lot of young people wear them all the time now.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：现在很多年轻人都时时刻刻穿着运动鞋。

transcript.en: They've become more popular than ordinary shoes.
transcript.cn: 它们比普通鞋子更受欢迎。

transcript.en: DON: I know. I guess they are very hard-wearing, but don't they look a bit casual for school uniform?
transcript.cn: 多恩：我知道。我想它们很耐磨，但作为校服，它们看起来是不是有点随意？

transcript.en: I don't think they're right for that.
transcript.cn: 我觉得不合适。

transcript.en: BELLA: Actually, I think some of them look quite smart on pupils . . . better than a scruffy old pair of shoes.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：其实，我觉得有些小学生穿上它们还挺好看的......总比穿一双邋遢的旧鞋好。

transcript.en: So do you keep shoes a long time?
transcript.cn: 那你的鞋子会穿很长时间吗？

transcript.en: DON: Yes. Though I do tend to wear my old pairs for doing dirty jobs like cleaning my bike.
transcript.cn: 多恩：是的。不过，我还是倾向于穿我的旧鞋去干脏活，比如擦我的自行车。

transcript.en: BELLA: I must admit, I've recycled some perfectly good shoes, that haven't gone out of fashion and still fit, just because they don't look great on me anymore. That's awful isn't it?
transcript.cn: 贝拉：我必须承认，我曾回收过一些很好的鞋子，它们还没有过时，也还合脚，只是因为它们穿在我身上不再好看了。这很糟糕，不是吗？

transcript.en: DON: I think it's common because there's so much choice.
transcript.cn: 多恩：我认为这很正常，因为有太多的选择了。

transcript.en: The article did say that recent sales of footwear have increased enormously.
transcript.cn: 这篇文章确实提到了最近鞋类的销售量有了很大的增长。

transcript.en: BELLA: That didn't surprise me.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：这并不让我感到惊讶。

transcript.en: DON: No. But then it said that the amount of recycled footwear has fallen: it's 6 percent now compared to a previous level of 11 percent.
transcript.cn: 多恩：嗯，但后来又说回收鞋类的数量下降了：现在是 6%，而以前是 11%。

transcript.en: That doesn't seem to make sense.
transcript.cn: 这似乎说不通。

transcript.en: BELLA: That's because not everything goes through the recycling process.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：那是因为并不是所有的东西都会经过回收处理。

transcript.en: Some footwear just isn't good enough to re-sell, for one reason or another, and gets rejected.
transcript.cn: 有些鞋类因为这样或那样的原因无法再销售，就会被拒收。

transcript.en: BELLA: So let's find some examples in the article of footwear that was rejected for recycling.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：那么，让我们在文章中找一些被拒绝回收的鞋类的例子。

transcript.en: DON: OK. I think there are some in the interview with the recycling manager. Yeah - here it is.
transcript.cn: 多恩：好的，我记得在对回收经理的采访中有一些。是的，在这里。

transcript.en: BELLA: Mmm. Let's start with the ladies' high-heeled shoes.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：嗯。我们从女士高跟鞋这里开始。

transcript.en: What did he say about those?
transcript.cn: 他是怎么说的？

transcript.en: DON: He said they were probably expensive - the material was suede and they were beige in colour - it looked like someone had only worn them once, but in a very wet field so the heels were too stained with mud and grass to re-sell them.
transcript.cn: 多恩：他说这双靴子可能很贵，材质是绒面革，颜色是米色的，看起来好像有人只穿过一次，不过是在非常潮湿的田野里，所以鞋跟上沾满了泥和草，无法再卖出去。

transcript.en: BELLA: OK . . . and the leather ankle boots. What was wrong with them?
transcript.cn: 贝拉：好吧......还有那双皮踝靴。有什么问题吗？

transcript.en: DON: Apparently, the heels were worn - but that wasn't the problem.
transcript.cn: 多恩：很明显，鞋跟磨损了，但这不是问题所在。

transcript.en: One of the shoes was a much lighter shade than the other one - it had obviously been left in the sun. I suppose even second-hand shoes should look the same!
transcript.cn: 其中一双鞋的颜色比另一双浅得多，显然是被太阳晒过的。我想即使是二手鞋看起来也应该是一样的！

transcript.en: BELLA: Sure. Then there were the red baby shoes.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：当然，还有那双红色的婴儿鞋。

transcript.en: DON: Oh yes - we're told to tie shoes together when we put them in a recycling bin, but people often don't bother.
transcript.cn: 多恩：哦，是的，我们被告知把鞋子放进回收箱时要绑在一起，但人们往往懒得这么做。

transcript.en: BELLA: You'd think it would have been easy to find the other, but it wasn't.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：你以为很容易就能找到另一只，但事实并非如此。

transcript.en: That was a shame because they were obviously new.
transcript.cn: 真可惜，因为它们显然是新的。

transcript.en: DON: The trainers were interesting.
transcript.cn: 多恩：这双运动鞋很有趣。

transcript.en: He said they looked like they'd been worn by a marathon runner.
transcript.cn: 他说看起来像是马拉松运动员穿过的。

transcript.en: BELLA: Yeah - weren't they split?
transcript.cn: 贝拉：是啊，这双鞋开胶了。

transcript.en: DON: Not exactly. One of the soles was so worn under the foot that you could put your finger through it.
transcript.cn: 多恩：不完全是。其中一个鞋底在脚下磨损得很厉害，手指都能穿过去。

transcript.en: Well, we could certainly use some of those examples in our presentation to explain why 90 percent of shoes that people take to recycling centres or bins get thrown into landfill.
transcript.cn: 好吧，我们肯定可以在我们的演讲中用这些例子来解释为什么人们带到回收中心或垃圾桶的90% 的鞋子都被扔进了垃圾填埋场。

transcript.en: BELLA: Mmm. What did you think about the project his team set up to avoid this by making new shoes out of the good parts of old shoes?
transcript.cn: 贝拉：嗯。他的团队设立了一个项目，用旧鞋的好部件制作新鞋，以避免这种情况的发生，你对这个项目怎么看？

transcript.en: DON: It sounded like a good idea. They get so many shoes, they should be able to match parts.
transcript.cn: 多恩：这听起来是个好主意。他们有那么多鞋，应该可以搭配起来。

transcript.en: I wasn't surprised that it failed, though. I mean who wants to buy second-hand shoes really? Think of all the germs you could catch!
transcript.cn: 不过，我对它的失败并不感到惊讶。我的意思是，谁会真的想买二手鞋呢？想想你能感染多少细菌！

transcript.en: BELLA: Well, people didn't refuse them for that reason, did they?
transcript.cn: 贝拉：好吧，人们不是因为这个原因拒绝他们的，不是吗？

transcript.en: It was because the pairs of shoes weren't identical.
transcript.cn: 那是因为每双鞋都不一样。

transcript.en: DON: They still managed to ship them overseas, though.
transcript.cn: 多恩：不过，他们还是设法把它们运到了海外。

transcript.en: BELLA: That's another area we need to discuss.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：这是我们需要讨论的另一个话题。

transcript.en: DON: You know I used to consider this topic just from my own perspective, by thinking about my own recycling behaviour without looking at the bigger picture.
transcript.cn: 多恩：你知道，我过去只是从自己的角度来考虑这个问题，只考虑自己的回收行为，而没有从大局出发。

transcript.en: So much happens once shoes leave the recycling area.
transcript.cn: 一旦鞋子离开回收区，就会发生很多事情。

transcript.en: BELLA: It's not as simple as you first think, and we can show that by taking a very different approach to it.
transcript.cn: 贝拉：这并不像你最初想的那么简单，我们可以用一种截然不同的方法来说明这一点。

transcript.en: DON: Absolutely. So let's discuss . . .
transcript.cn: 多恩：当然。那么，让我们来讨论一下......

groups.title: Tardigrades
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: • more than 1,000 species, 0.05–1.2 millimetres long • also known as water ‘bears’ (due to how they $21702$) and ‘moss piglets’ Physical appearance • a $21703$ round body and four pairs of legs • claws or $21704$ for gripping • absence of respiratory organs • body filled with a liquid that carries both $21705$ and blood • mouth shaped like a $21706$ with teeth called stylets Habitat • often found at the bottom of a lake or on plants • very resilient and can exist in very low or high $21707$ Cryptobiosis • In dry conditions, they roll into a ball called a ‘tun’. • They stay alive with a much lower metabolism than usual. • A type of $21708$ ensures their DNA is not damaged. • Research is underway to find out how many days they can stay alive in $21709$. Feeding • consume liquids, e.g., those found in moss or $21710$. • may eat other tardigrades Conservation status • They are not considered to be $21711$.

questions.analysis: 听力材料中明确解释了“water bears”这个名字的由来是因为它们的移动方式。通过抓住关键词“water bears”并听取后面的解释，考生可以准确地找到答案。
答案是“short”，因为题干要求填入一个描述身体外观的形容词。听力原文中提到“a body which is short, and also rounded”，其中“short”是描述身体的形容词，符合题干要求。因此，正确答案是“short”。
题干中的定位词是“claws”，在听力原文中提到“some species don't have any claws; what they have are discs”，这句话明确指出了答案“discs”。题干要求填入一个单词来描述用于抓握的结构，而“discs”正是这种结构，因此答案是“discs”。
答案是“oxygen”，因为在听力原文中明确提到“oxygen and also blood are transported in a fluid that fills the cavity of the body”，即氧气和血液通过充满身体腔体的液体进行运输。这与题干中的描述完全吻合。
答案是“tube”，因为听力原文明确提到“tardigrade's mouth is a kind of tube”，这与题干中的“mouth shaped like a”相对应。题干要求填入嘴巴的形状，而原文中直接描述了嘴巴的形状是“tube”。
答案是“temperatures”，因为听力原文明确提到水熊虫能够在极低和极高的温度下生存。题干中的“low or high”是关键的定位词，帮助我们在听力中快速找到相关信息。学生在听力过程中应特别注意这些定位词，并根据上下文判断正确答案。

答案是“protein”，因为听力原文明确提到“While in a state of cryptobiosis, tardigrades produce a protein that protects their DNA.”。题干中的定位词是“Cryptobiosis”和“DNA”，信号词也是这两个词。即使学生基础较差，只要能识别出这些定位词和信号词，并在听力过程中集中注意力，就能较容易地捕捉到答案。

答案是“space”，因为听力原文中明确提到“tests taking place in space, to determine how long tardigrades might be able to survive there”，这与题干中的“Research is underway to find out how many days they can stay alive in”相对应。通过定位词“Research”、“days”、“stay alive”和信号词“tests”、“survive”、“how long”，可以准确找到答案。
题干中提到“consume liquids, e.g., those found in moss or 【    】”，其中“moss”是定位词。在听力原文中，出现了“moss”这个词，紧接着提到了“seaweed”，这就是我们需要的答案。因为题干要求填入的是一种液体来源，而“seaweed”正是符合这个要求的答案。
正确答案是“endangered”，因为听力原文明确提到“tardigrades... are not on any endangered list”，这直接对应题干中的“not considered to be”。通过定位词“Conservation status”和信号词“not on any endangered list”，可以准确找到答案所在的句子。

transcript.en: PART 4
For my project on invertebrates, I chose to study tardigrades.
transcript.cn: 在我的无脊椎动物项目中，我选择了研究缓足虫。

transcript.en: These are microscopic - or to be more precise - near-microscopic animals.
transcript.cn: 这种动物是微生物，或者更准确地说是近微生物。

transcript.en: There are well over a thousand known species of these tiny animals, which belong to the phylum Tardigrada.
transcript.cn: 这种微小的动物有一千多个已知物种，属于缓步动物门。

transcript.en: Most tardigrades range in length from 0.05 to 1 millimetre, though the largest species can grow to be 1.2 millimetres in length.
transcript.cn: 大多数蜥脚类动物的长度在 0.05 到 1 毫米之间，不过最大的种类可以长到 1.2 毫米。

transcript.en: They are also sometimes called 'water bears' : 'water' because that's where they thrive best, and 'bear' because of the way they move.
transcript.cn: 它们有时也被称为 "水熊"："水 "是因为它们在水里生长得最好，而 "熊 "则是因为它们移动的方式很像。

transcript.en: 'Moss piglet' is another name for tardigrades because of the way they look when viewed from the front.
transcript.cn: “苔藓小猪”是缓足虫的另一个名字，因为从正面看它们的样子很像。

transcript.en: They were first discovered in Germany in 1773 by Johann Goeze, who coined the name Tardigrada.
transcript.cn: 它们最早于1773年在德国被约翰 · 戈泽发现，他创造了“缓步动物”这个名字。

transcript.en: As I say, there are many different species of tardigrade - too many to describe here - but, generally speaking, the different species share similar physical traits.
transcript.cn: 正如我所说，缓足虫有许多不同的种类——太多了，这里无法一一描述——但是，一般来说，不同的物种有着相似的生理特征。

transcript.en: They have a body which is short, and also rounded - a bit like a barrel - and the body comprises four segments.
transcript.cn: 它们有一个短而圆的身体——有点像桶——身体由四个部分组成。

transcript.en: Each segment has a pair of legs, at the end of which are between four and eight sharp claws.
transcript.cn: 每一节都有一对腿，末端有四到八只锋利的爪子。

transcript.en: I should also say that some species don't have any claws; what they have are discs, and these work by means of suction.
transcript.cn: 另外，一些物种没有爪子；它们有的只是圆盘，这些圆盘通过吸力工作。

transcript.en: They enable the tardigrade to cling on to surfaces or to grip its prey.
transcript.cn: 它们使缓足虫能够附着在表面或者抓住猎物。

transcript.en: Within the body, there are no lungs, or any organs for breathing at all. Instead, oxygen and also blood are transported in a fluid that fills the cavity of the body.
transcript.cn: 在它的体内，没有肺，也没有任何用于呼吸的器官。相反，氧气和血液在充满体腔的液体中运输。

transcript.en: As far as the tardigrade's head is concerned, the best way I can describe this is that it looks rather strange - a bit squashed even - though many of the websites I looked at described its appearance as cute, which isn't exactly very scientific.
transcript.cn: 就缓足虫的头而言，我能描述的最好的方式就是它看起来很奇怪——甚至有点被压扁了——尽管我看过的许多网站描述它的外观很可爱，这并不是很科学。

transcript.en: The tardigrade's mouth is a kind of tube that can open outwards to reveal teeth-like structures known as 'stylets'.
transcript.cn: 缓足动物的嘴是一种管状物，可以向外张开，露出被称为“口针”的牙齿状结构。

transcript.en: These are sharp enough to pierce plant or animal cells.
transcript.cn: 这些结构非常锋利，足以刺穿植物或动物细胞。

transcript.en: So, where are tardigrades found?
transcript.cn: 那么，缓足虫是在哪里被发现的呢？

transcript.en: Well, they live in every part of the world, in a variety of habitats: most commonly, on the bed of a lake, or on many kinds of plants or in very wet environments.
transcript.cn: 嗯，它们生活在世界的每一个角落，栖息地多种多样: 最常见的是，在湖床上、多种植物上或在非常潮湿的环境中。

transcript.en: There's been some interesting research which has found that tardigrades are capable of surviving radiation and very high pressure, and they're also able to withstand temperatures as cold as-200 degrees centigrade, or highs of more than 148 degrees centigrade, which is incredibly hot.
transcript.cn: 有一些有趣的研究发现缓足虫能够在辐射和高压下生存，而且它们还能够承受零下200摄氏度的低温，或者超过148摄氏度的高温，这是难以忍受的高温。

transcript.en: It has been said that tardigrades could survive long after human beings have been wiped out, even in the event of an asteroid hitting the earth.
transcript.cn: 据说缓足虫可以在人类灭绝之后存活很长时间，即使是在小行星撞击地球的情况下。

transcript.en: If conditions become too extreme and tardigrades are at risk of drying out, they enter a state called cryptobiosis.
transcript.cn: 如果环境变得过于极端，缓足虫面临干涸的危险，它们就会进入隐生状态。

transcript.en: They curl into a ball, called a tun - that's T-U-N - by retracting their head and legs, and their metabolism drops to less than one percent of normal levels.
transcript.cn: 它们把头和腿缩成一团，称为 "桶"（Tun），新陈代谢下降到正常水平的百分之一以下。

transcript.en: They can remain like this until they are reintroduced to water, when they will come back to life in a matter of a few hours.
transcript.cn: 它们可以保持这种状态，直到重新接触到水，几小时后，它们就会恢复生机。

transcript.en: While in a state of cryptobiosis, tardigrades produce a protein that protects their DNA.
transcript.cn: 当处于隐生状态时，缓足虫产生一种蛋白质来保护它们的DNA。

transcript.en: In 2016, scientists revived two tardigrades that had been tuns for more than 30 years. 2016
transcript.cn: 年，科学家们复活了两种已经存在了30多年的缓足虫。

transcript.en: There was a report that, in 1948, a 120-year-old tun was revived, but this experiment has never been repeated.
transcript.cn: 有报道说，在1948年，一只120岁的缓足虫复活了，但是这个实验从来没有被重复过。

transcript.en: There are currently several tests taking place in space, to determine how long tardigrades might be able to survive there.
transcript.cn: 目前有几项测试正在太空中进行，以确定缓足虫能在那里生存多久。

transcript.en: I believe the record so far is 10 days.
transcript.cn: 到目前为止的记录是10天。

transcript.en: So, erm, moving on. In terms of their diet, tardigrades consume liquids in order to survive.
transcript.cn: 所以，继续，从它们的饮食来看缓足虫为了生存会摄入液体。

transcript.en: Although they have teeth, they don't use these for chewing.
transcript.cn: 虽然它们有牙齿，但它们不用这些来咀嚼。

transcript.en: They suck the juices from moss, or extract fluid from seaweed, but some species prey on other tardigrades, from other species or within their own.
transcript.cn: 它们从苔藓中吸取汁液，或者从海藻中提取液体，但是一些物种会捕食其他的缓足虫，从其他物种或在它们自己的物种中摄入汁液。

transcript.en: I suppose this isn't surprising, given that tardigrades are mainly comprised of liquid and are coated with a type of gel.
transcript.cn: 我想这并不奇怪，因为缓足虫主要是由液体组成，并且被一种凝胶包裹着。

transcript.en: Finally, I'd like to mention the conservation status of tardigrades.
transcript.cn: 最后，我想提一下缓足虫的保护现状。

transcript.en: It is estimated that they have been in existence for approximately half a billion years and, in that time, they have survived five mass extinctions.
transcript.cn: 据估计，它们已经存在了大约5亿年，在此期间，它们经历了5次大灭绝。

transcript.en: So, it will probably come as no surprise to you, that tardigrades have not been evaluated by the International Union for Conservation of Nature and are not on any endangered list.
transcript.cn: 因此，你可能不会感到惊讶，缓步动物没有被国际自然保护联盟评估过，也没有被列入任何濒危物种名单。

transcript.en: Some researchers have described them as thriving.
transcript.cn: 一些研究人员称它们正在茁壮成长。

transcript.en: Does anyone have any questions they'd like to ask?
transcript.cn: 大家有什么问题想问吗？
